source.notes: Optimus _ CAT Global - 2026_04_09 12_27 EDT - Notes by Gemini.docx

call_summary: Optimus's founder/CEO Ed Stockman and AE Max Riemer pitched their data-and-AI freight-prospecting platform to CAT Global's Columbus brokerage team — Director of Sales Shawn and his rep Dom. After roughly a third of the call spent on soccer/career rapport, they demoed lane-mapping, contact, and overlap features against CAT's real PetSmart lane and agreed to a three-user, 60-credit trial starting Monday. Shawn was warm but repeatedly flagged he has no buying authority (decisions come from corporate), so the deal hinges on a warm handoff, while Dom — the actual frontline user — asked the sharpest evaluative questions.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Ed was consistently responsive and conversational rather than scripted, engaging directly with the buyers' backgrounds and steering the demo off their real accounts; his warmth ran well above his own baseline (valence z +2.3), with only a mild arousal fade late in the call.

evidence.quote: I love these examples. I like talking through the real world stuff.
Pets Smart's the number one ship right now. Yeah. I I wanted to make sure that that data was right.

value_prop_clarity.explanation: Ed framed a clear, concrete why-care — more data and a custom value prop before the call, tied to a fast ROI story where account managers pay the system back in the first week.

evidence.quote: how do we help companies make more money and close more shippers
you do that with your account management team for the first week and the system's paid for
Optimus' goal is to be able to give your reps far more data and a custom value proposition

relevance.explanation: He tailored the pitch to CAT's structure and used their live business — confirming PetSmart (their just-won, top shipper) and speaking to their hybrid account-management model and Mastery TMS.

evidence.quote: Pets Smart's the number one ship right now.
For the amount of money that we're charging you guys, you do that with your account management team for the first week and the system's paid for.

discovery_progression.explanation: Ed asked productive qualifying questions (revenue, headcount, account ownership, TMS) and landed a dated next step, though he never converted the discovery that Shawn can't buy into a path to the corporate decision-maker.

evidence.quote: what what does the brokerage do in annual revenue roughly?
How many how many sales people?
Max, just set them up with um like 60 credits, 20 a piece between the three of them

Ed Stockman.coaching_action: Once Shawn told you buying decisions come only from corporate, don't stop at a trial with a non-buyer — secure a concrete, dated next step to get in front of the corporate decision-maker (a named intro or joint call) before investing the trial.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Max was responsive and let the buyers drive the demo with their own lane, so this is not a tone penalty; his energy did run notably below his own baseline (arousal z -1.7, fading to the end) and the demo needed several corrections from Ed.

evidence.quote: Is there like a like a specific lane you'd want to look up?
could you give us sort of like the the quick overhead of cat

value_prop_clarity.explanation: During the demo Max mostly narrated the UI and hedged the payoff, leaving Ed to supply the actual 'why this matters'; from Max's words alone a neutral listener would struggle to state the one-sentence reason to care.

evidence.quote: you know start sourcing back all opportunities if that's uh part of the game plan there for your carriers
All the inbound points into the DFW area there.

relevance.explanation: Max grounded the demo in CAT's own request, filtering Dom's Fort Worth–to–Bethel PA lane for PetSmart rather than a generic canned example.

evidence.quote: Is there like a like a specific lane you'd want to look up?
I'm going to do within 50 miles. So we filter Fort Worth to PA 236 companies

discovery_progression.explanation: Max owned the mechanics of moving forward — proposing trial options and pinning down a start date and follow-up.

evidence.quote: I told Sean we could do three days of unlimited or like a week long with credits
you think tomorrow is a good start date just so you can have a full day whenever you start?

Max Riemer.coaching_action: Rehearse the demo so every screen opens with a one-sentence 'here's why this matters for CAT's lanes/carriers' before you show the feature, instead of narrating the UI and leaving the value framing to Ed.

signals.signal: Warmed from initial skepticism to willingness to try
signals.quote: at first I think I think you guys called me a couple times. I was like, "Ah, I don't think this really super valuable for us." Um but, uh I mean, the more I kind of thought about it

signals.signal: Explicitly limited authority — caps his commitment to a warm handoff
signals.quote: my power is basically limited to I can give you a very warm, you know, handoff essentially

signals.signal: Self-excludes as an active user, diluting his own buy-in
signals.quote: with me not using it quite as much as the other guys. Um, I don't do a lot of direct selling anymore

signals.signal: Product-usage question showing genuine consideration of trialing it
signals.quote: Do we have a a limit on the amount of like searches per day type of thing

signals.signal: Sharp differentiation question probing real-world sales edge
signals.quote: are there any other tools that you guys can provide that gives us like salespeople like sort of an edge like when you go into a call

signals.signal: Probed depth of the data to judge usefulness for his workflow
signals.quote: do we have the names of those companies of like their suppliers and their customers or is it just just the lane?

signals.signal: Recognized value in his own words
signals.quote: Okay, because those are also potential customers as

signals.signal: Brought a live, high-stakes account to test against
signals.quote: we just got a big award from PetSmart and uh it's uh not ideal timing
